articles.title: DESIGNER NEWS IS DEAD
articles.summary: Designer News, once a prominent hub for product designers, has officially shut down, marking the end of its unique, text-focused discussions on nuanced aspects of product design. Initially thriving and exclusive, the platform deteriorated after opening registration to everyone, leading to a decline in post quality and an influx of spam.

articles.title: TESLA’S CARS JUST GOT ‘RECALLED’ OVER A FONT
articles.summary: A recent recall from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) on Tesla cars was prompted by a very peculiar reason: the font on the touchscreen display is too small. The NHTSA says people might have trouble seeing the ⅛-inch warning sign for the automaker's brake system. It claims that the international standard for that text is 3.2 millimeters and anything less may pose a safety risk. The issue, which affects 2.2 million cars, can be fixed with a simple software update.

articles.title: THE APPLE VISION PRO IS A REVOLUTION IN AR/VR INTERFACES
articles.summary: The Apple Vision Pro represents a significant advancement in mixed-reality interface design. It utilizes visionOS to seamlessly integrate eye and hand tracking for intuitive control without needing external devices. This interface offers unparalleled accessibility and ease, making it the most user-friendly AR/VR experience to date.

articles.title: KEY TAKEAWAYS FROM AIRBNB’S WINTER REDESIGN
articles.summary: Airbnb’s latest launch shows us how great designers can develop simple solutions to the two big pain points: it created the “Guest favorite” feature to increase users’ trust and reorganized the listings tab to improve the hosts’ listing experience.

articles.title: THE PROBLEM WITH DIGITAL INSTRUMENT CLUSTERS AND HOW TO DESIGN A BETTER ONE
articles.summary: Most new cars today have digital instrument clusters without physical constraints, which should be a benefit, but most carmakers use it to add unnecessary complexity. Clusters should adapt to different contexts, for example, they should be simple and controlled by the driver when driving at night.

articles.title: CARD SORTING: UNCOVER USERS' MENTAL MODELS FOR BETTER INFORMATION ARCHITECTURE
articles.summary: The card sorting study is a tool in which users organize topics into groups to create an information architecture that suits their expectations. Designers can pick between open, closed, or hybrid card sorting, moderated or unmoderated, and in-person or remote.

articles.title: SITEMAP BUILDER AND WEBSITE PLANNER
articles.summary: Octopus enables you to create website maps or application structures, add notes, specify page content, and use color schemes to improve your site map design or implement website development or content planning.

articles.title: WEBSITE DESIGN INSPIRATION
articles.summary: With InspoVault, you can discover and collect live website design inspiration and trends.

articles.title: VISION PRO TEARDOWN—WHY THOSE FAKE EYES LOOK SO WEIRD
articles.summary: Apple wanted to achieve something particular when it designed the EyeSight feature for its Vision Pro: an animated, 3D-looking face with eyes. It had to make very strategic design choices and compromises to accomplish this. Yet, the result is a slightly creepy animation that requires another screen, more connectors, and points of failure.

articles.title: ART OF SYMBOLS
articles.summary: Symbols are integral to our design vocabulary, so we must know where they come from and what they mean. This website teaches you about the meaning of many symbols over the past 40,000 years and how they were used throughout human history.

articles.title: USING SF SYMBOL RENDERING MODES WHEN DESIGNING YOUR APP
articles.summary: Apple's icon set, SF Symbols, features four distinct rendering modes with different colors and appearances: monochrome, hierarchical, palette, and multicolor. These rendering modes and icon features make designs using SF symbols feel infinitely customizable.

articles.title: EXPLORING 3D TYPOGRAPHY WITH BOGDAN DREAVA'S ALPHABET ART
articles.summary: Dive into 3D and typography through Bogdan Dreava's Alphabet Illustrations. Discover how depth and shadows bring letters to life in a monochrome palette.

articles.title: WHY GOOD UX IS NO UX
articles.summary: The evolution of car keys, from basic mechanical devices to sophisticated, app-based systems, mirrors the progression towards more intuitive and frictionless user experiences. Good UX design is unobtrusive and integrates seamlessly into daily life, making technology feel like a natural extension of our routines.

articles.title: USING AI FOR 3D RENDERING—A PRACTICAL GUIDE FOR DESIGNERS
articles.summary: The slow, meticulous process of creating high-quality renders is often incompatible with the fast pace required from early concept ideation. On the other hand, the ease of iterating with AI-generated images is unmatched - even a slight tweak in a prompt can lead to a significant shift in visual style.
